title: Spike - Review and Enhance End User Settings
story: As a product administrator, I want to understand the relationship between individual end-user settings and team-level permissions, so that I can decide whether to deprecate, enhance, or suppress user-level show/hide controls in favor of account-level management.
description:
Spike to evaluate whether end-user settings should be deprecated in favor of team-level account settings. The original user-level show/hide tab controls were not functioning correctly and may conflict with the newer team-level permissions system where admins can turn tabs on/off.

The decision was to keep the Settings page but add Categories and Branding to it rather than fully deprecating. Future personalization (language, defaults, etc.) will live on the Settings page while tab visibility remains an account-level admin control.
acceptance_criteria: Review end-user settings vs team-level permissions
Add Categories and Branding sections to the Settings page
Ensure no conflict between user-level and team-level settings
ux_changes: Settings page enhanced with Categories and Branding sections. Tab visibility controlled at the account/team level.
middleware_changes: Settings page updated to integrate categories and branding management.
implementation_notes: The spike evolved from 'deprecate settings' to 'enhance settings.' Individual personalization settings are preserved for future use (language, defaults), while tab show/hide is managed at the account level via admin permissions.
comment_summary: Product decided not to deprecate but to review and enhance. Categories and Branding added to the Settings page. Developer completed on development.
